Application submission behavior matches documented business rules and an explanatory message is shown to the user.

test_cases.test_case: Upload maximum number of allowed files and total size near system limit
preconditions: User is logged in and on the visa application form
System has defined limits for number of attachments and total upload size
steps: 1. Upload the maximum number of allowed document files (e.g., passport copy, photograph, invitation letter, multiple supporting docs) using supported formats
2. Ensure combined file sizes are near but do not exceed the allowed total size
3. Submit the application
test_cases.expected_result: All files are accepted and listed; application submits successfully; files remain attached and downloadable from application details. If limits are exceeded, the UI shows a clear error describing the specific limit breached.

test_cases.test_case: View My Bookings list and verify booking details and action buttons
preconditions: User is logged in
User has at least one past and one upcoming booking across any service type
steps: 1. Navigate to the User Dashboard
2. Click the 'My Bookings' section
3. Observe the bookings list and click through a sample past booking card and a sample upcoming booking card
test_cases.expected_result: Bookings list displays booking reference, service type, travel dates, status; appropriate action buttons (View Details, Cancel, Modify, Download) are visible per booking; clicking a booking opens its details page

test_cases.test_case: Download confirmation/invoice/voucher for a confirmed booking
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a 'Confirmed' booking that includes downloadable documents
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > My Bookings
2. Locate a confirmed booking
3. Click the Download/Invoice/Voucher button for that booking
test_cases.expected_result: A PDF or appropriate document is downloaded or opened in a new tab containing booking details and invoice/confirmation; file name and contents reflect the booking reference

test_cases.test_case: Cancel an eligible upcoming booking following cancellation policy
preconditions: User is logged in
User has an upcoming booking that is cancellable per policy and within allowed time window
steps: 1. Open Dashboard > My Bookings
2. Select an eligible upcoming booking
3. Click the Cancel button
4. Confirm cancellation in the confirmation dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Booking status updates to 'Cancelled'; user sees a confirmation message and downloadable cancellation confirmation; cancellation is recorded in transaction history if applicable

test_cases.test_case: Edit profile information and save changes
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard > My Profile
2. Click Edit
3. Change First Name, Last Name, and Phone number to valid new values
4. Click Save
test_cases.expected_result: Profile is updated; a success notification is shown; updated values display in the profile view

test_cases.test_case: View wallet balance and transaction history with filters
preconditions: User is logged in
User has wallet/credits and transaction history
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > Wallet/Credits
2. Verify displayed available credit balance
3. Open Full Transaction History and apply a date range filter and a transaction-type filter
4. Inspect a transaction detail entry
test_cases.expected_result: Available balance is accurate; transaction history loads and filters correctly; transaction detail modal or view shows date, amount, type, and reference

test_cases.test_case: Add an item to Wishlist and view it
preconditions: User is logged in
There is at least one hotel/tour/flight available to add
steps: 1. From the site listing or details page, click Add to Wishlist for a hotel/tour/flight
2. Navigate to Dashboard > Wishlist
3. Verify the added item appears with correct details and link to the item
test_cases.expected_result: Item appears in Wishlist with title, thumbnail, service type and link; user can click through to the item details

test_cases.test_case: Submit a rating and review for a completed booking
preconditions: User is logged in
User has at least one completed booking eligible for review
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > Reviews
2. Select a completed booking and choose a rating (stars)
3. Enter a review title and body with valid text
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Review is accepted; a success message is shown; the review appears in the user's review list and is reflected in the booking details if applicable

test_cases.test_case: Change account password with correct current password
preconditions: User is logged in
User knows current password
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard > Settings > Change Password
2. Enter the correct current password, a new valid password, and confirm the new password
3. Click Save/Change Password
test_cases.expected_result: Password is changed; a success notification is shown; subsequent login requires new password

test_cases.test_case: Logout ends the session and redirects to public landing page
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Click the Logout button in Dashboard or header
2. Confirm logout if a confirmation is shown
test_cases.expected_result: User is logged out, session cleared, and redirected to the home or login page; protected dashboard pages are no longer accessible without logging in

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to cancel a non-cancellable booking
preconditions: User is logged in
User has an upcoming booking that is marked non-cancellable by the policy
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard > My Bookings
2. Locate the non-cancellable booking
3. Click the Cancel button (if shown) or attempt cancellation via booking details
test_cases.expected_result: System prevents cancellation and displays an inline error or modal explaining the cancellation policy and reason; booking remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to download an invoice when document is missing
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking that does not have an attached invoice/confirmation
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > My Bookings
2. For the booking without documents, click the Download/Invoice button
test_cases.expected_result: System shows an appropriate error or message (e.g., 'Document not available') and does not trigger a download

test_cases.test_case: Save profile with invalid email format
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on Dashboard > My Profile
steps: 1. Click Edit on profile
2. Enter an invalid email (e.g., 'user@@domain' or 'userdomain.com')
3. Click Save
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Email field; changes are not saved until a valid email is provided

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit a review for an upcoming (not completed) booking
preconditions: User is logged in
User has an upcoming booking (not completed)
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > Reviews
2. Attempt to select the upcoming booking to leave a review or open review form from booking details
3. Enter rating and review text and click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: System blocks review submission for non-completed bookings and shows an explanatory message (e.g., 'You can only review completed bookings')

test_cases.test_case: Change password using incorrect current password
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard > Settings > Change Password
2. Enter an incorrect current password and a valid new password and confirmation
3. Click Save/Change Password
test_cases.expected_result: System rejects the change and shows an error indicating the current password is incorrect; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Access protected dashboard pages after logout (session should be invalid)
preconditions: User has logged out from the application
steps: 1. Attempt to navigate to Dashboard URL or click Back in browser to access dashboard pages
2. Try to perform an action (e.g., open My Bookings) without logging in
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page and cannot view or perform actions on dashboard pages until authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Load My Bookings when user has a very large number of bookings (pagination & performance)
preconditions: User is logged in
User account has hundreds/thousands of bookings across services
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard > My Bookings
2. Scroll through the list, use pagination or 'Load More' controls, and perform a search/filter on bookings
3. Click through to several booking details
test_cases.expected_result: Bookings load incrementally or via pagination without UI breaking; filters and searches work; performance is acceptable and no data is missing or duplicated

test_cases.test_case: Submit an extremely long review text (boundary/max length)
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a completed booking eligible for review
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > Reviews
2. Select a completed booking and enter a very long review text (e.g., 50,000 characters) and rating
3. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: System either accepts the review (with truncation) or rejects it with a clear validation error about maximum length; UI remains stable and no data corruption occurs

test_cases.test_case: Apply wallet credit when available balance is zero
preconditions: User is logged in
User wallet balance is zero
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard > Wallet/Credits
2. Attempt to apply wallet credit to a new booking or check 'Apply Credit' option if available
3. Observe system response
test_cases.expected_result: System prevents applying credits or shows an informative message ('Insufficient wallet balance'); no negative balances are created and UI clearly indicates zero balance

test_cases.test_case: Change default currency setting to an invalid or extremely long value
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on Dashboard > Settings > Preferences
steps: 1. Open Settings > Default Currency
2. Attempt to enter/select an invalid currency code or paste a very long/invalid string
3. Click Save
test_cases.expected_result: System rejects invalid input with validation error or sanitizes input; settings are not saved with invalid currency; UI shows supported currency list if applicable

test_cases.test_case: Display wishlist items that have been removed/unavailable
preconditions: User is logged in
User has items in Wishlist, some of which were removed from inventory or became unavailable
steps: 1. Go to Dashboard > Wishlist
2. Inspect an item that is no longer available and click through to its detail
3. Attempt to book or view the removed item
test_cases.expected_result: Unavailable wishlist items show a clear status ('No longer available') and provide an appropriate action (remove from wishlist or notify when available); clicking the item does not lead to an error page

test_cases.test_case: View booking detail page shows full information
preconditions: User is logged in
User has at least one confirmed booking with confirmation number <confirmation_number>
steps: 1. Navigate to My Bookings / Reservations
2. Click the booking with confirmation number <confirmation_number> to open the detail view
test_cases.expected_result: Booking detail view displays the confirmation number, full service information (dates, itinerary or service details), traveler details, payment information (last4, amount paid), and current booking status (Confirmed/Pending/Cancelled). All fields are populated and legible.

test_cases.test_case: Successfully modify booking dates when policy and availability allow
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> that is modifiable per its cancellation/modification policy
New requested dates have availability
steps: 1. Open the booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click the Modify button
3. Change the travel dates to <new_check_in> and <new_check_out>
4. Review any shown modification fees or additional charges and accept them
5. Submit the modification
test_cases.expected_result: The booking details update to reflect the new dates; booking status shows 'Modified' (or similar) with updated modification timestamp; any adjustment to payment is shown and charged/refunded accordingly and a success message is displayed. An email notification about the modification is sent to the booking email address.

test_cases.test_case: Add special requests and update traveler information via Modify
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> that allows modification of traveler info and special requests
steps: 1. Open the booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Modify
3. Update traveler information (e.g., change passenger last name, add DOB where allowed)
4. Add a special request in the Special Requests field (e.g., 'Vegetarian meal')
5. Save changes
test_cases.expected_result: Traveler information and special requests are saved and reflected on the booking detail page; a confirmation message is displayed and an email notification about the updated booking details is sent.

test_cases.test_case: Cancel a refundable booking and initiate refund to original payment method
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> that is cancellable and partially or fully refundable according to policy
steps: 1. Open the booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Cancel
3. Review the cancellation confirmation flow which shows the applicable refund amount and any fees
4. Confirm the cancellation when prompted
test_cases.expected_result: Booking status updates to 'Cancelled' (or equivalent) and a cancellation confirmation message is shown; refund is initiated to the original payment method for the displayed amount; user sees refund initiation/transaction reference if available; an email notification about the cancellation and refund is sent to the booking email.

test_cases.test_case: Receive email notifications on booking confirmation, modification and cancellation
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the email account used for booking
Booking events (confirmation, modification, cancellation) are triggered for <confirmation_number> during the test
steps: 1. Confirm a new booking or trigger a booking confirmation for <confirmation_number>
2. Modify the booking (if applicable) or trigger a modification event
3. Cancel the booking (if applicable) or trigger a cancellation event
4. Check the booking email inbox or system email logs for emails corresponding to each event
test_cases.expected_result: An email is received for each status change: confirmation email upon booking creation, modification email after changes, and cancellation email after cancellation. Each email contains the confirmation number, updated status, and relevant details (dates, refund amount if cancelled).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to modify a booking when modification is disallowed by policy
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> with a non-modifiable policy (e.g., non-changeable fare)
steps: 1. Open the booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Modify
test_cases.expected_result: The Modify action is disabled or the system displays a clear inline message stating modifications are not allowed due to policy; no changes can be submitted and no charges or emails are generated.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to modify booking dates to dates with no availability
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a modifiable booking <confirmation_number>
Requested new dates are unavailable
steps: 1. Open booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Modify
3. Select new dates <unavailable_start> to <unavailable_end>
4. Attempt to submit the change
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents the change and displays an error message indicating no availability for the selected dates; the booking remains unchanged and no fees or emails are generated.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to cancel a non-cancellable or non-refundable booking
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> that is non-cancellable or non-refundable per policy
steps: 1. Open the booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Cancel
3. Review the cancellation confirmation flow
test_cases.expected_result: The UI indicates cancellation is not permitted (or shows zero refund and indicates non-refundable); if Cancel is disabled the user cannot proceed. No cancellation is processed and no refund or cancellation email is sent.

test_cases.test_case: Do not confirm cancellation in confirmation flow — booking should remain active
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a cancellable booking <confirmation_number>
steps: 1. Open booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Cancel
3. When the cancellation confirmation flow appears showing refund amount, click Cancel/Close on the dialog or click 'No' instead of confirming
test_cases.expected_result: Booking remains in its original status (e.g., Confirmed); no cancellation is processed, no refund is initiated, and no cancellation email is sent. The cancellation dialog closes and the booking detail view remains accessible.

test_cases.test_case: Modify booking on the boundary day (last allowed change before departure)
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> with policy that allows changes up to <boundary_hours> before departure
Current time is within the test window at or exactly <boundary_hours> before the service start
steps: 1. Open booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Modify
3. Attempt to change dates or other modifiable details exactly at the boundary cutoff time
4. Submit the modification
test_cases.expected_result: If policy allows modifications at the boundary, the modification is accepted and processed with any applicable fees; booking shows 'Modified'. If policy disallows modifications at the boundary, the system prevents the change and shows a clear message explaining the cutoff. No inconsistent state is produced.

test_cases.test_case: Cancel booking where the displayed refund amount is zero due to non-refundable fees
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> that has cancellation fees equal or greater than paid amount resulting in zero refund
steps: 1. Open booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Cancel
3. Review the cancellation flow showing refund amount of 0.00
4. Confirm the cancellation
test_cases.expected_result: Booking status updates to 'Cancelled' and the UI clearly shows that refund amount is 0.00 due to fees; no refund transaction is initiated but a cancellation confirmation and details (including fees applied) are shown and emailed to the user.

test_cases.test_case: Add extremely long special request text and ensure storage/display/truncation behavior
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a booking <confirmation_number> that allows special requests
steps: 1. Open booking detail view for <confirmation_number>
2. Click Modify
3. Enter a special requests text of length > 5000 characters (or the system's documented maximum) and save
4. Re-open the booking detail view
test_cases.expected_result: System either accepts and stores the request showing full text where supported, or enforces a documented maximum length with a clear validation message. No data corruption occurs; UI displays the stored/allowed portion of the request and system emails include the same stored content.

test_cases.test_case: Successful Credit Card payment (Visa) without saving card
preconditions: User has selected a booking and is on the Payment page
User has a valid Visa card with sufficient funds
SSL/security badges are displayed on the page
steps: 1. Verify the booking summary and price breakdown is visible
2. Select Credit/Debit Card as payment method and choose Visa
3. Enter valid Cardholder Name, valid 16-digit Visa Card Number, future Expiration Date, and 3-digit CVV
4. Ensure Save Card for future use is unchecked
5. Click Pay / Submit Payment
test_cases.expected_result: Payment is processed successfully; user is redirected to booking confirmation page showing reference number, options to download invoice/voucher; a confirmation email is sent; no card is saved to the account

test_cases.test_case: Successful PayPal payment flow
preconditions: User has selected a booking and is on the Payment page
User has a valid PayPal account and credentials
steps: 1. Verify booking summary and totals are displayed
2. Select PayPal as payment method
3. Click Proceed to PayPal
4. Complete authentication and payment on the PayPal sandbox page
5. Return to the site when redirected after successful payment
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected back to the booking confirmation page with reference number and invoice/voucher download options; confirmation email is sent; booking details remain intact if user navigates back

test_cases.test_case: Successful Wallet/Credits payment
preconditions: User is logged in and has sufficient wallet/credits balance
User is on the Payment page with booking selected
steps: 1. Verify booking summary and price breakdown
2. Select Wallet/Credits as payment method
3. Confirm usage of wallet balance and click Pay
4. Observe balance deduction and completion
test_cases.expected_result: Payment completes using wallet credits; user is shown booking confirmation page with reference number and invoice/voucher options; wallet balance is reduced accordingly and confirmation email is sent

test_cases.test_case: Bank Transfer selection provides instructions and pending booking state
preconditions: User has selected a booking and is on the Payment page
steps: 1. Verify booking summary and totals
2. Select Bank Transfer as payment method
3. Click Continue or Confirm to receive bank transfer instructions
4. Observe on-screen transfer instructions and booking status
test_cases.expected_result: User is shown bank transfer details and reference to include; booking moves to a pending state indicating confirmation upon receipt of funds; options to download invoice/voucher (if applicable) and instruction email are sent

test_cases.test_case: Save card option stores card for future use and displays in saved cards
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Payment page
User has a valid card to enter
steps: 1. Enter valid card details (cardholder name, number, expiry, CVV)
2. Check Save Card for future use option
3. Complete payment successfully
4. Navigate to User Profile -> Saved Cards or make a new booking and open Payment page
test_cases.expected_result: Card token or masked card appears in the user's saved cards list; on a subsequent booking the saved card can be selected as a payment method and used for quick payment

test_cases.test_case: Payment rejected for invalid card number (fails Luhn check)
preconditions: User is on the Payment page with booking selected
steps: 1. Select Credit/Debit Card payment
2. Enter Cardholder Name and an invalid card number that does not pass Luhn (e.g., incorrect digits)
3. Enter valid expiry and CVV
4. Click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation prevents submission or payment gateway returns an error; user sees an explicit validation/error message about invalid card number; booking details remain visible for correction

test_cases.test_case: Payment blocked for expired card (expiration date in the past)
preconditions: User is on the Payment page
steps: 1. Select Credit/Debit Card payment
2. Enter valid Cardholder Name and Card Number
3. Enter Expiration Date that is in the past
4. Enter valid CVV and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error indicates the card is expired and payment is rejected; user can correct the expiration date or choose another payment method without losing booking details

test_cases.test_case: CVV length mismatch for American Express card
preconditions: User is on the Payment page and selects American Express as card type
steps: 1. Enter valid Amex card number and Cardholder Name
2. Enter a 3-digit CVV instead of required 4-digit CVV
3. Enter valid expiry and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation shows a CVV format/length error specific to American Express; payment is not processed until correct 4-digit CVV is provided

test_cases.test_case: Payment fails due to card declined / insufficient funds and user can retry
preconditions: User is on the Payment page
Test card configured to simulate 'Card declined' or 'Insufficient funds' response
steps: 1. Enter test card details that trigger decline
2. Click Pay
3. Observe error message and retry option
4. Correct the payment method or enter a different card and attempt payment again
test_cases.expected_result: User sees a clear error message such as 'Card declined' or 'Insufficient funds'; booking details and entered form data are retained so user can retry or switch payment method without re-entering booking information

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit card form with missing Cardholder Name
preconditions: User is on the Payment page
steps: 1. Select Credit/Debit Card payment
2. Leave Cardholder Name empty and fill other fields with valid data
3. Click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation highlights Cardholder Name as required and prevents submission; user sees a message indicating the field is mandatory

test_cases.test_case: Process payment for a very large booking total to verify price breakdown and totals
preconditions: User has selected a booking with a very large total amount (e.g., high-value corporate booking)
User is on the Payment page
steps: 1. Verify price breakdown displays base price, taxes, service fees, discounts, and total with correct arithmetic
2. Select any supported payment method (card or wallet) and complete payment using valid credentials
3. Observe confirmation and reference number
test_cases.expected_result: System correctly displays and processes the large monetary values without rounding errors or overflow; payment completes and confirmation page shows accurate totals; invoice reflects the same breakdown

test_cases.test_case: Enter an extremely long Cardholder Name containing 255 characters
preconditions: User is on the Payment page
steps: 1. Select Credit/Debit Card payment
2. Enter a Cardholder Name with 255 characters (letters, spaces)
3. Fill other required card fields with valid data and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: System accepts or properly truncates the long name according to spec without errors; payment proceeds or a clear validation message appears if length limits are exceeded; booking details are preserved

test_cases.test_case: Network interruption during payment submission preserves booking details and allows retry
preconditions: User is on the Payment page with booking selected
Test environment can simulate network drop during payment submission
steps: 1. Enter valid card or payment credentials
2. Trigger payment submission and simulate network interruption mid-request
3. Restore network connectivity and retry the payment or select a different method
test_cases.expected_result: When network is lost, user sees an appropriate error/notification; booking and previously entered payment details are preserved (or tokenized) and user can retry without re-entering booking details; no duplicate bookings are created

test_cases.test_case: Cardholder Name with unusual special characters and unicode
preconditions: User is on the Payment page
steps: 1. Select Credit/Debit Card payment
2. Enter Cardholder Name containing unicode characters and special symbols (e.g., accents, emojis)
3. Enter valid card number, expiry, CVV and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: System either accepts the unicode/special characters and processes payment or shows a clear validation message specifying allowed characters; no system crash or data corruption occurs

test_cases.test_case: Change currency (unauthenticated) on search results page updates prices and preserves search context
preconditions: User is not logged in (unauthenticated)
User is on the Hotels search results page with a destination, check-in/check-out dates and guest selection already applied
steps: 1. Note current displayed currency (e.g., USD) and selected search criteria (destination, dates, guests)
2. Click the Currency selector in header
3. Select 'EUR' from the currency list
4. Observe page auto-refresh or dynamic update
test_cases.expected_result: All displayed prices on the page update to EUR using the correct conversion rates; search criteria (destination, dates, guests) remain unchanged and result list stays for the same search. A session/cookie is set to store the selected currency for the session.

test_cases.test_case: Change language (unauthenticated) in header switches entire UI to Spanish and stores preference in session
preconditions: User is not logged in (unauthenticated)
User is on the homepage
steps: 1. Click the Language selector in header
2. Select 'Español (Spanish)' from the language list
3. Observe the page reload or dynamic text replacement across the UI
test_cases.expected_result: All navigation labels, form labels, buttons and most UI content are displayed in Spanish; layout remains functionally identical; a session/cookie is set to store the language for the current browsing session.

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user changes currency and language; preferences are saved to profile and persist after logout/login
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on any page (e.g., account dashboard)
steps: 1. Click the Currency selector and choose 'GBP'
2. Click the Language selector and choose 'French (Français)'
3. Log out of the application
4. Log back in with the same user account
test_cases.expected_result: After selection, site shows prices in GBP and UI in French. After logout and subsequent login, the site defaults to GBP and French for that user account (preferences persisted in profile).

test_cases.test_case: Change currency during checkout recalculates totals and retains booking inputs
preconditions: User has a booking in progress (hotel room selected) and is on the booking/checkout page
Booking form fields (guest info, payment selection) are filled
steps: 1. Note the current currency and order total (including taxes and fees)
2. Use the currency selector on the checkout page to switch to another currency (e.g., from USD to AUD)
3. Observe the price recalculation and UI updates
test_cases.expected_result: Order subtotal, taxes, fees and grand total are recalculated and displayed in the selected currency; all booking form inputs remain populated and no data is lost. The conversion is reflected on any price breakdowns and final payable amount.

test_cases.test_case: Switch to Arabic (RTL) updates layout to right-to-left and translates UI elements
preconditions: User is on the homepage or any internal page
steps: 1. Click the Language selector
2. Select 'Arabic (العربية)'
3. Observe page layout, alignment and text direction
test_cases.expected_result: The entire site switches to Arabic text where translations exist; page layout switches to right-to-left (RTL), navigation alignment, form fields and icons mirror appropriately, and content remains accessible and usable.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to set currency via manipulated URL/parameter to an unsupported currency code
preconditions: User is on any page
Tester has ability to append or modify currency parameter in URL (e.g., ?currency=XXX)
steps: 1. Append an unsupported currency code parameter to the URL (e.g., ?currency=ZZZ) and load the page
2. Observe how the site handles the invalid currency code
test_cases.expected_result: The site ignores the invalid currency code or falls back to the default currency; no prices are shown with corrupt formatting. If an error is surfaced, it is a user-friendly message indicating unsupported currency. No application or UI breakage occurs.

test_cases.test_case: Change currency/language with cookies disabled — preference not persisted and warning shown
preconditions: User has cookies disabled in browser settings
User is on the homepage
steps: 1. Click Currency selector and choose a different currency
2. Click Language selector and choose a different language
3. Navigate to another page or refresh the site
4. Observe persistence of the selected preferences
test_cases.expected_result: Preferences (currency and language) apply immediately for the current page if possible, but are not persisted across page reloads or navigation; the site displays a clear notification or banner informing the user that preferences cannot be saved without cookies and suggesting enabling cookies for persistence.

test_cases.test_case: Switch to a language that lacks translations for some dynamic content — placeholders or fallback appear
preconditions: User is on a page that contains dynamic/third-party content (e.g., vendor descriptions, aggregated reviews)
steps: 1. Open Language selector and choose a language that is partially supported by the system
2. Observe pages containing dynamic content or third-party widgets
test_cases.expected_result: Areas without translations either fall back to the default language (e.g., English) or show a graceful placeholder (no broken tags); no raw keys or template placeholders are visible. If fallback occurs, a non-intrusive note may indicate partial translation.

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly switch currencies multiple times to ensure UI stability and consistent rates
preconditions: User is on a price-heavy page (search results or booking breakdown)
steps: 1. Click the Currency selector
2. Switch between 4-5 different currencies in quick succession (e.g., USD -> EUR -> JPY -> GBP -> AUD) within a short time window
3. Observe price updates, formatting and any UI glitches
test_cases.expected_result: Prices update correctly each time with appropriate formatting for each currency; there is no residual mixing of currencies, no frozen UI, no duplicated price elements, and no JavaScript errors preventing selection. Final selected currency is honored and persisted appropriately for the session or user.

test_cases.test_case: Change language on a deep workflow page (payment confirmation/booking details) and verify content translation and state preservation
preconditions: User has progressed to a deep workflow page (e.g., payment page or booking confirmation page) with populated form fields and/or order summary
steps: 1. On the deep workflow page, click the Language selector and choose a different language (e.g., French)
2. Observe UI translation and confirm that all form entries and order summary values remain unchanged
3. Attempt to submit or navigate further in the workflow after language change
test_cases.expected_result: UI text and labels change to the selected language while all entered data, booking selections and order summary values remain intact; the workflow can continue or be completed without loss of state. Date/time, number and currency formats adapt as per selected locale where applicable.

test_cases.test_case: Select a currency with zero/minimal decimals (e.g., JPY) and verify correct formatting and rounding behavior
preconditions: User is on a product or booking page showing price breakdowns
steps: 1. Open Currency selector and choose Japanese Yen (JPY) or another currency that typically uses no decimals
2. Observe price displays, taxes and totals across the page and in any popups or emails (if available)
test_cases.expected_result: Prices are formatted correctly without unnecessary decimal places (e.g., ¥1,234 not ¥1,234.00); rounding rules are applied consistently across subtotal, taxes and total. No mismatched decimals or calculation errors appear.

test_cases.test_case: Apply price range slider on Hotels listing and verify dynamic results and count
preconditions: User is on the Hotels listing page
Search results are displayed with multiple hotels
steps: 1. Locate the Price Range slider in the left sidebar
2. Drag the minimum handle to increase the minimum price to a mid-range value
3. Drag the maximum handle to decrease the maximum price to a mid-range value
4. Observe the results grid and result count while moving the handles
test_cases.expected_result: Results update dynamically as the slider is adjusted; result count at the top updates to reflect the number of hotels within the chosen price range; displayed hotels' prices fall within the selected range

test_cases.test_case: Filter hotels by amenities and star rating
preconditions: User is on the Hotels listing page
Hotel filters section is visible
steps: 1. Expand the Facilities/Amenities filter section
2. Check 'Free WiFi' and 'Swimming Pool' amenities
3. Expand the Star Rating filter and select 4 stars and above
4. Watch the results update and check the Active Filters summary
test_cases.expected_result: Results are filtered to hotels that advertise Free WiFi and a Swimming Pool and have 4 stars or higher; Active Filters summary lists the selected amenities and star rating; result count updates accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Apply flight filters: airline, number of stops and departure time range
preconditions: User is on the Flights listing page
Search has returned multiple flight options
steps: 1. In the Airlines filter, select a single airline (e.g., Airline A)
2. In the Stops filter, select 'Non-stop' or 'Direct' option
3. Set the Departure Time range slider to morning hours (e.g., 06:00 - 12:00)
4. Observe the results and Active Filters summary
test_cases.expected_result: Results show only flights operated by Airline A with non-stop options departing between 06:00 and 12:00; result count and Active Filters summary reflect the three applied filters

test_cases.test_case: Change sorting control to sort by price ascending and descending
preconditions: User is on any listing page (e.g., Hotels) with multiple results
Sorting control is visible above the results grid
steps: 1. Open the Sort dropdown
2. Select 'Price: Low to High'
3. Verify the top results are the lowest priced items
4. Open the Sort dropdown again and select 'Price: High to Low'
5. Verify the top results are the highest priced items
test_cases.expected_result: When 'Price: Low to High' is selected, results are ordered with lowest prices first; when 'Price: High to Low' is selected, results are ordered with highest prices first; the sorting selection persists until changed

test_cases.test_case: Remove an individual active filter from the Active Filters summary
preconditions: User is on a listing page with at least two active filters applied
Active Filters summary is visible at the top of the results
steps: 1. Identify the Active Filters summary showing applied filters
2. Click the remove (x) button on one active filter (e.g., 'Free WiFi')
3. Observe the results grid and result count after removal
test_cases.expected_result: The chosen filter is removed from the Active Filters summary; results update dynamically to reflect removal; result count increases or changes appropriately and remaining filters remain applied

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Reset all filters' control to clear all filters and restore full results
preconditions: User is on a listing page with multiple filters applied
Active Filters summary and 'Reset all filters' control are visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Reset all filters' control/button in the Active Filters area
2. Observe the filter sidebar and Active Filters summary
3. Verify the results grid and result count after reset
test_cases.expected_result: All filters in the sidebar are cleared (checkboxes unchecked, sliders reset); Active Filters summary is empty; results grid returns to the unfiltered default list and result count reflects full result set

test_cases.test_case: Collapse and expand filter sections and apply a filter while collapsed/expanded
preconditions: User is on any listing page
Left sidebar displays collapsible filter sections
steps: 1. Collapse the 'Location/Area' filter section
2. Expand the 'Facilities/Amenities' section and select one amenity
3. Expand the previously collapsed 'Location/Area' section and select one area
4. Observe results updating and check that the collapsed state persists when navigating within the listing
test_cases.expected_result: Collapsing and expanding works without losing selections; selecting a filter in an expanded section applies correctly; results update dynamically and maintain UI state appropriately

test_cases.test_case: Apply Car-specific filters (transmission, car type, rental company) and verify results
preconditions: User is on the Cars listing page
Multiple car listings are available
steps: 1. Select 'Automatic' under the Transmission filter
2. Select a specific Car Type (e.g., 'SUV')
3. Check a specific Rental Company from the list
4. Verify the results and Active Filters summary
test_cases.expected_result: Results contain only cars matching Automatic transmission, the selected car type, and the selected rental company; Active Filters summary lists the applied car-specific filters and the result count updates accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Filter tours by tour type and duration range
preconditions: User is on the Tours listing page
Tours listing contains varied durations and types
steps: 1. Expand the Tour Type filter and select 'Adventure' (or equivalent)
2. Set Duration range to 1-3 days using the duration control
3. Observe results and Active Filters summary
test_cases.expected_result: Results show only tours labeled 'Adventure' with durations between 1 and 3 days; Active Filters summary reflects tour type and duration filters; result count updates appropriately

test_cases.test_case: Apply multiple filters across categories and verify combined filtering behavior
preconditions: User is on the Hotels listing page
There are many available hotels to filter
steps: 1. Select a Location/Area
2. Set Price Range to a narrow band
3. Select 3-star rating
4. Choose at least one facility (e.g., 'Parking')
5. Observe resulting listings and Active Filters summary
test_cases.expected_result: Results reflect the intersection of all applied filters (area, price range, star rating, facility); Active Filters summary lists all applied filters individually; result count shows number matching all criteria

test_cases.test_case: Set flight departure time range with end time earlier than start time (invalid range)
preconditions: User is on the Flights listing page
Departure Time range control accepts start and end values
steps: 1. Set the Departure Time start to 18:00
2. Set the Departure Time end to 08:00 (earlier than start)
3. Attempt to apply the filter
test_cases.expected_result: The UI prevents applying an invalid time range and shows an inline validation message near the time control (or automatically normalizes to a valid range); results do not change while invalid values persist

test_cases.test_case: Manually input Price minimum greater than maximum and expect validation
preconditions: User is on a listing page where price can be set via numeric inputs or slider handles with manual value entry
steps: 1. Enter 1000 as Minimum Price
2. Enter 100 as Maximum Price (less than minimum)
3. Click outside the control or try to apply the filter
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating the minimum cannot be greater than the maximum (or the inputs are auto-corrected); filters are not applied until valid values are entered

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to set a negative tour duration via manual input
preconditions: User is on the Tours listing page
Duration filter allows manual numeric input
steps: 1. Enter -2 in the Minimum Duration field
2. Enter 3 in the Maximum Duration field
3. Attempt to apply the filter
test_cases.expected_result: System rejects the negative duration with inline validation indicating duration must be a positive number; filter is not applied until corrected

test_cases.test_case: Set price slider to extreme min and max values (full range) and verify results
preconditions: User is on a listing page with price slider that supports full-range selection
steps: 1. Drag the price slider minimum handle to the minimum allowed value (e.g., 0)
2. Drag the price slider maximum handle to the maximum allowed value
3. Observe the results grid and Active Filters summary
test_cases.expected_result: All results are shown (effectively unfiltered by price) and result count equals the total available; Active Filters summary may show the price filter set to full range or not show price if treated as default; UI handles extreme values without errors

test_cases.test_case: Apply a combination of filters that yields zero results and verify no-results UI and reset suggestion
preconditions: User is on any listing page with diverse data
Multiple filters are available
steps: 1. Apply several restrictive filters across categories (e.g., remote area + very high minimum price + specific rare amenity)
2. Observe the results area after filters are applied
3. Check for No Results messaging and available actions (e.g., Reset all filters, suggestions)
test_cases.expected_result: Listing shows a clear 'No results' message with possible reasons or suggestions; Active Filters summary still lists all applied filters; a prominent 'Reset all filters' or suggestion to broaden the search is offered and clicking it returns results

test_cases.test_case: Select all available filters (maximum) and verify Active Filters summary handles overflow and individual removals
preconditions: User is on a listing page with many filter options (numerous checkboxes)
Active Filters summary has limited horizontal space
steps: 1. Check every available checkbox filter across multiple filter sections
2. Observe the Active Filters summary at the top for truncation, overflow, or grouping behavior
3. Attempt to remove a single filter from the summary (choose one not visible due to truncation if present)
test_cases.expected_result: Active Filters summary gracefully handles many filters (e.g., shows a summarized count or wraps lines) without UI overlap; each filter remains removable individually (including those not initially visible via an expanded summary); results update accordingly when a single filter is removed

test_cases.test_case: Aggregate rating and review count displayed on listing pages
preconditions: User is on a listing page (e.g., hotels or tours) with items that have reviews
steps: 1. Open the listing page for hotels
2. Observe the summary area on each item card
test_cases.expected_result: Each item card displays an aggregate rating score (e.g., 4.5) and total review count (e.g., 123); values reflect backend data and are clickable to navigate to detail page

test_cases.test_case: Detail page shows Reviews section with full review details
preconditions: User is on the detail page of an item (hotel/tour) that has at least one review
steps: 1. Scroll to the Reviews section on the item's detail page
2. Inspect an individual review entry
test_cases.expected_result: Reviews section lists individual reviews including overall star rating, category-specific ratings (e.g., Cleanliness, Service, Location), reviewer name and country, review date, stay date, written comments, and any guest-uploaded photos; photos are viewable in a lightbox or larger view

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user with completed booking submits a valid review via dashboard
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a completed booking eligible for review
User is on the user Dashboard > My Bookings > Leave Review for the completed booking
steps: 1. Click Leave Review for the eligible booking from the dashboard
2. Select an overall star rating (e.g., 5 stars)
3. Provide category-specific ratings (e.g., Cleanliness 5, Service 4, Location 5)
4. Enter written feedback in the comments field
5. Upload one or more valid image files (optional)
6. Submit the review
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is accepted; user sees a success confirmation message and the new review appears in the detail page Reviews section with correct ratings, comments, reviewer name and country, stay date, and photos

test_cases.test_case: Filter reviews by rating, date range, and traveler type
preconditions: Detail page contains multiple reviews with varying ratings, dates, and traveler types
steps: 1. Open the Reviews section on the detail page
2. Apply a filter for 5-star ratings
3. Apply a date range filter (e.g., last 12 months)
4. Apply a traveler type filter (e.g., Couple)
5. Clear individual filters and apply combined filters
test_cases.expected_result: Review list updates after each filter application to show only reviews matching the selected rating, date range, and traveler type; combined filters narrow results accordingly; clearing filters restores the full list

test_cases.test_case: Submit review via post-stay email prompt link
preconditions: User received a post-stay review invitation email and is logged in (or logs in when prompted)
Booking referenced in email is marked as completed and eligible for review
steps: 1. Click the review link in the post-stay email
2. If prompted, log in with the account that completed the booking
3. Provide overall and category-specific ratings
4. Enter comments and upload optional photos
5. Submit the review
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the review submission page; after submitting, a confirmation message is shown and the review is visible on the item's detail page

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to submit a review
preconditions: User is not logged in
User navigates to a detail page for an item they have booked but is not authenticated in the session
steps: 1. Click Leave a Review or Submit Review on the detail page
2. Attempt to access the review submission form
test_cases.expected_result: System prompts the user to log in or sign up before allowing review submission; review form is not accessible until authentication is completed

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user without completed booking attempts to submit a review
preconditions: User is logged in
User has an account but has no completed/eligible bookings for the item
steps: 1. Navigate to the item detail page
2. Click Leave a Review or review submission link
3. Attempt to fill and submit a review
test_cases.expected_result: System blocks submission and displays an error/message indicating the user is not eligible to review this item (e.g., 'Only guests who completed a stay can leave a review'); no review is created

test_cases.test_case: Submit review with missing required overall rating
preconditions: User is logged in and eligible to review (completed booking)
User is on the review submission form
steps: 1. Leave the overall rating unselected or set to zero
2. Fill category-specific ratings and enter comments
3. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error is shown indicating the overall rating is required; form is not submitted until a valid overall rating is selected

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to upload non-image files as guest-uploaded photos
preconditions: User is logged in and on the review submission form
User attempts to upload files
steps: 1. Click upload photo and select a non-image file (e.g., .exe or .pdf)
2. Attempt to submit the review
test_cases.expected_result: Upload is rejected with an inline error indicating invalid file type; only image files are accepted and review submission fails until invalid files are removed or replaced

test_cases.test_case: Submit review with maximum-length comment and maximum allowed photos
preconditions: User is logged in and eligible to review
System has defined maximum comment length and maximum number of photos
steps: 1. Open the review form for an eligible booking
2. Enter a comment equal to the maximum allowed characters (boundary)
3. Upload the maximum allowed number of valid image files
4. Provide required ratings and submit
test_cases.expected_result: System accepts the review if limits are not exceeded; comment is stored exactly up to the maximum length and all photos are uploaded and displayed; if any hard limits are exceeded an appropriate error is shown preventing submission

test_cases.test_case: Submit review with stay date set in the future
preconditions: User is logged in and on the review submission form
Booking record incorrectly contains a future stay date or user manually edits stay date field if allowed
steps: 1. Open review form and set the stay date to a future date (if editable) or attempt to submit if system shows future stay date
2. Provide ratings and comments
3. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: System validates stay date; either it accepts submission if business rules allow (and shows the future stay date in the review) or it rejects with an error stating stay date must be on or before the review date; behavior is consistent with business rules and shows clear messaging

test_cases.test_case: Apply a filter date range that yields no reviews
preconditions: Detail page has reviews but none within the chosen out-of-range date window
steps: 1. Open Reviews section
2. Apply a date range filter that does not include any existing review dates (e.g., future dates or a very narrow past range)
3. Observe the results area
test_cases.expected_result: Review list displays an appropriate 'No reviews found' message or empty state illustration; filter controls remain available to change or clear the filter

test_cases.test_case: Load Offers page and display promotional banners and deal cards
preconditions: User is on the website and navigates to the Offers & Deals page
steps: 1. Open the Offers & Deals page from the main navigation
2. Observe the top area for promotional banners
3. Scroll to view featured deal cards, last-minute offers, and seasonal packages
test_cases.expected_result: Page loads successfully; promotional banners are visible; multiple deal cards are displayed showing deal title, image, discount or special rate, validity period, Terms and Conditions link, and a Book Now button for each card

test_cases.test_case: Filter offers by service type, destination and travel dates
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
steps: 1. Select 'Hotels' as the service type filter
2. Enter 'Paris' in the Destination field
3. Select check-in date and check-out date within valid range
4. Click the Apply/Search button for filters
test_cases.expected_result: Offer list refreshes to show only deals applicable to Hotels in Paris within the selected dates; each displayed deal indicates validity period and any discounted rate

test_cases.test_case: Click Book Now on a hotel deal applies promotional code automatically
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
There is at least one active hotel deal with an auto-apply promo code
steps: 1. Identify a hotel deal card labeled with an auto-apply promo or discount
2. Click the Book Now button on that deal card
3. Proceed to the booking flow (review booking page or checkout)
test_cases.expected_result: User is taken into the booking flow (either a pre-filled search or booking page) and the promotional code is automatically applied (visible in the price breakdown or promo code field); discounted rates are reflected in totals

test_cases.test_case: Book Now redirects to a pre-filled flight search with discounted rates
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
There is an active flight deal intended to redirect to pre-filled search
steps: 1. Locate a flight deal card and note the departure/arrival and date information
2. Click the Book Now button
3. Observe the resulting page for search parameters and prices
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to a flight search/listing page with departure, arrival and dates pre-filled; listed fares reflect the deal's discounted rates or a promo code is automatically shown/applied

test_cases.test_case: Subscribe to newsletter with a valid email
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page and newsletter subscription field is visible
steps: 1. Enter a valid email address (e.g., test.user+offers@example.com) into the newsletter subscription field
2. Click the Subscribe/Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: A success message is displayed confirming subscription (e.g., 'Thank you for subscribing'); email is accepted and no inline error remains

test_cases.test_case: Open Terms and Conditions link for a deal
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page and deal cards are visible
steps: 1. Locate a deal card and click its Terms and Conditions link
2. Observe whether terms open as a modal, new page, or in-page expansion
3. Read the content and close the modal or return to the offers list
test_cases.expected_result: Terms and Conditions content opens and is readable; closing the content returns the user to the Offers page without losing filter state or scroll position

test_cases.test_case: Subscribe to newsletter with invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page and newsletter subscription field is visible
steps: 1. Enter an invalid email such as 'invalid-email' into the newsletter subscription field
2. Click the Subscribe/Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error is shown indicating invalid email format (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email'); subscription is not accepted

test_cases.test_case: Click Book Now for a deal that is expired or has no current availability
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
There exists a deal card whose validity period has passed or whose inventory is sold out
steps: 1. Identify the expired or sold-out deal card
2. Click the Book Now button for that deal
test_cases.expected_result: An informative message is displayed indicating the deal is no longer available or no availability for the selected dates; the user is not taken to a booking with an applied promo and is offered alternative suggestions if available

test_cases.test_case: Apply filter with check-out date before check-in date
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
steps: 1. Select a service type and destination
2. Set check-in date to a later date (e.g., 2026-07-10)
3. Set check-out date to an earlier date (e.g., 2026-07-08)
4. Click Apply/Search
test_cases.expected_result: Validation prevents applying the filter; an inline error message is shown (e.g., 'Check-out date must be after check-in date') and no filtered results are returned until dates are corrected

test_cases.test_case: Attempt newsletter subscription with empty email field
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page and newsletter subscription field is visible
steps: 1. Leave the newsletter email field empty
2. Click the Subscribe/Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation message is shown indicating the field is required (e.g., 'Please enter your email'); subscription is not processed

test_cases.test_case: Search offers with same-day check-in and check-out (zero-night stay / same-day travel)
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
steps: 1. Select a service type (e.g., Packages or Flights)
2. Set check-in (or travel) date and check-out date to the same calendar day
3. Apply filters and view results
test_cases.expected_result: System returns offers valid for same-day travel if supported (e.g., one-day packages or same-day flights) or shows a clear message if same-day stays are not supported; no system error occurs

test_cases.test_case: Filter by an extremely long destination string
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
steps: 1. Paste a very long string (e.g., 500+ characters) into the Destination field
2. Click Apply/Search
test_cases.expected_result: The system either truncates input with client-side validation or returns no results without crashing; an appropriate validation message is shown if applicable and UI remains responsive

test_cases.test_case: Verify display of extreme discount values (0% and 100%) on deal cards
preconditions: There are deal cards with discount displayed as 0% and another with 100% (free) configured in the system
steps: 1. Find and view the deal card showing 0% discount
2. Find and view the deal card showing 100% discount
3. Click Book Now on both cards to observe behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Deal cards display percentages correctly (0% and 100%); clicking Book Now either applies the correct pricing (no discount for 0%, free for 100%) or shows a clear explanation of pricing; no formatting errors appear

test_cases.test_case: Apply multiple filters that return no matching offers
preconditions: User is on the Offers & Deals page
steps: 1. Select Service: Flights
2. Enter a niche destination with unlikely matches (e.g., small remote island)
3. Select travel dates far in the past or far in the future
4. Click Apply/Search
test_cases.expected_result: The page shows a clear 'no results' message with suggestions (e.g., broaden filters or clear filters); UI does not break and filter controls remain editable so the user can adjust criteria

test_cases.test_case: Standard logout via header/profile menu
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on any authenticated page (e.g., dashboard or profile)
steps: 1. Click the user profile/avatar or account menu in the header
2. Click the 'Logout' button/link
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the home page; authenticated UI elements (e.g., profile name, account links) are no longer visible; login/register controls are shown; session-related cookies/storage are cleared or invalidated

test_cases.test_case: Logout using mobile/responsive menu
preconditions: User is logged in
Device viewport is narrow (mobile) or responsive menu is used
steps: 1. Open the mobile/responsive menu or hamburger menu
2. Tap the 'Logout' option
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the home page; mobile menu closes; authenticated items are removed and login/register options are displayed; session is terminated

test_cases.test_case: Access protected page by direct URL after logout
preconditions: User is logged in
User performs logout and is redirected to home page
steps: 1. In the address bar, enter the URL of a protected page (e.g., /account/dashboard) after logout
2. Press Enter to navigate
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page (or shown the login modal); access to the protected page is denied until the user logs in again

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back button after logout to attempt to view a protected page
preconditions: User is logged in
User navigated to a protected page and then clicks Logout
steps: 1. After logout and redirection to home, click the browser Back button
2. Observe the page shown
test_cases.expected_result: Browser does not display the protected page content; user is redirected to the login page or shown an authentication required message; cached protected data is not accessible

test_cases.test_case: Logout in one tab then refresh protected page in another tab
preconditions: User is logged in in two separate browser tabs
Protected page is open in Tab A; Tab B will be used to logout
steps: 1. In Tab B, perform logout (click Logout and confirm redirection to home)
2. Switch to Tab A and refresh the protected page (press F5 or click Reload)
test_cases.expected_result: Tab A does not display protected content after refresh; it redirects to the login page or shows authentication required; session is not active in Tab A

test_cases.test_case: Click Logout while an in-flight background request (AJAX) is in progress
preconditions: User is logged in
A background operation that communicates with the server is active (e.g., saving profile changes or submitting a form via AJAX)
steps: 1. Start the background operation (trigger the AJAX request)
2. Immediately click the Logout button before the background request completes
test_cases.expected_result: The application terminates the session and redirects to the home page; any in-flight requests are either cancelled or return an authentication error; no protected UI remains visible; user is required to log in again to continue

test_cases.test_case: Trigger logout multiple times (click Logout repeatedly or call logout endpoint twice)
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Click the Logout button/link once
2. If the logout control remains visible or clickable, click Logout again (or open the logout URL directly a second time)
test_cases.expected_result: System handles the repeated logout gracefully (idempotent): user remains on or is redirected to the home page; no error pages or stack traces are shown; login/register options are available
